content:
- ES & NQ FUTURES
This session provides a quick review of the ES and NASDAQ Futures daily charts. We observed price
running outside of an imbalance and trading up into a judo swing before moving lower, as outlined in the
previous night's review. The objective was to see an upside movement to set up this judo swing.
Context for daily bias involves looking for a weekly drawn liquidity to frame a buy or sell program. Each day,
we await a Power of 3 to materialize.
For a bearish bias on S&P and NASDAQ:
- The open will be near the high.
- Any price movement above the opening price is viewed as a judo swing.
- A judo swing is a fake rally designed to entice traders to go long, followed by an aggressive move lower.
On both ES and NASDAQ, sell-side liquidity was resting below the observed price action.

- S&P (ES) AND NASDAQ (NQ)
On the hourly chart for S&P, we previously identified a buy-side imbalance outside of efficiency. Price
worked up to the top of that range and then broke lower, framing the daily high by tearing into the
inefficiency. Following a shift in market structure, price rallied back up into a fair value gap. Despite
movement beyond it, candle bodies remained very close to this shaded area.
This behavior is characteristic of August, which tends to be less clean in price delivery compared to other
months.
Key observations for S&P hourly:
- Buy-side imbalance outside of efficiency.
- Price worked up to the top of the range, broke lower.
- Market structure shift.
- Rally into a fair value gap.
- Small inefficiency (Fair Value Gap / SIBI) identified for charting.
- Anticipating the next sell cycle in close proximity.
For NASDAQ hourly, price traded up into consequent encroachment on the 15th, then broke lower. This
included:
- Consequent encroachment of a SIBI.
- Order block.
- Consequent encroachment of the buy-side imbalance on efficiency.
- A fair value gap where candle bodies reflected today's price action.
This is not typical support and resistance, but rather an inefficiency combined with wicks doing damage,
trading into the inefficiency and bearish order block. The candle bodies told the narrative, converting the
imbalance into a balanced price range (up-down-up-down half consequent encroachment). Price hit this
area like a 'brick wall' and sold off. Further indications included a fair value gap, consequent encroachment
of w wick, and tilt-closed candles.
At the 2 PM FOMC minutes, a significant sell-off was expected, gravitating towards 14853.5 for NASDAQ.

- ES & NQ
On the 15-minute chart, S&P traded into the shaded area outlined previously, then broke lower with a shift
in market structure. This movement is a direct application of the ICT Mentorship Model 2022 taught on
YouTube, delivered specifically in the afternoon during the FOMC minutes. The market accelerated to the
downside, targeting the 4424 level.
For NASDAQ on the 15-minute chart:
- An imbalance or fair value gap was present from Monday's 10 AM 60-minute buy-side imbalance / sell
- side inefficiency high and low.
- Price traded up into this area, demonstrating significant confluence.
- The market showed displacement to the downside, indicating a shift in market structure.
- Two fair value gaps were identified, allowing for price to trade up into the second one.
During FOMC minutes, price traded into a 15-minute sell-side imbalance / buy-side efficiency with
consequent encroachment for the gap, followed by an aggressive sell-off. An institutional overflow entry
drill, which is a move just above a specific candle high, preceded the sell-off. The target remained 14853.5.

The 5-minute chart review focused on the afternoon PM session, specifically the Silver Bullet trade setup.
Price traded up into the identified area and aggressively moved lower, attacking the 4424 level.
Key insights from the 5-minute chart:
- Inefficiencies are less apparent on the 5-minute timeframe when dropping down from 15 minutes.
- The presence of higher timeframe PD Arrays on charts is crucial to avoid getting lost in candlesticks and
price delivery.
- Price traded up, broke lower, with bodies forming inside the Monday 9:30 AM low and the Sunday 11:50
PM low these were mentioned as sell-side liquidity pools at the start of the trading day.
- The entire movement around 9:30 AM was a judo swing.
When a price objective is reached (often marked by 'Boom' or 'There it is' on Twitter), it serves as an
emotional stimulus and a milestone for journaling observations of outlined analyses. Critics who dismiss
initial rallies as insignificant miss the point: the market moves up to specific, pre-outlined levels (judo swing)
before reversing.
For bearish daily bias, ICT students know to expect an initial, opposing delivery (a false movement in the
opposite direction). This does not negate the analysis; it confirms the setup. Retail traders often
misinterpret these initial rallies as continuation, when in fact, they are part of a larger, predicted bearish
narrative.

section_title: CULTIVATING TRADING ACUMEN & THE ROLE OF TIME
content:
It is critical to follow ICT's Twitter for real-time insights. Tweets referencing specific levels can be copied and
pasted onto TradingView charts, attaching the comment to the exact candlestick at the time of the tweet.
This allows for verification of the outlined visibility.
Learning ICT concepts requires:
- Daily Practice: Condition your understanding and activate your reticular activating system to spot these
patterns.
- Journaling: Build affinity towards annotations on the chart. Seeing things in hindsight and then anticipating
them through studying in a tape-reading environment.
- Tape Reading Environment: This means not trading in demo, funded accounts, or live funds. Simply relax
and allow understanding to build over time without rushing to make money.
This growth phase is the longest but is essential for understanding expected price action. Every price run
and delivery mechanism is linked to time; nothing is random. The only exceptions are unexpected
geopolitical events or warfront developments, which would still be exploited by market makers. This
framework provides an undeniable, transferable skill for predicting market movements.
